description:
As the artifact-curation manager you need a one-shot health check that shows
    how much space the main binary repository currently consumes.

    1. Repository path  
       • /home/user/repos/binrepo

    2. Where to write the result  
       • /home/user/reports/binrepo_disk_usage.log

    3. Exact log-line format (single line, **no extra spaces, no line-breaks before or after**)  
       TIMESTAMP=<UTC_ISO8601> SIZE_BYTES=<integer>
         • TIMESTAMP must be expressed in UTC and follow the strict
           ISO-8601 basic form **YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ**  
           (example: 2024-03-31T14:07:59Z)  
         • SIZE_BYTES must be obtained from the command  
           du -sb /home/user/repos/binrepo | cut -f1  
           (i.e., a single integer representing the total bytes reported by
           GNU du; do not include explanatory text).

    4. Practical constraints  
       • Produce the file in exactly one terminal invocation or a compact
         pipeline — no intermediate files.  
       • Overwrite any existing /home/user/reports/binrepo_disk_usage.log.  
       • After the command finishes, a cat of the file must show precisely one
         line that matches the format above.

    The automated grader will:
       a) Read the single line inside /home/user/reports/binrepo_disk_usage.log.  
       b) Verify that the TIMESTAMP matches the required UTC ISO-8601 pattern.  
       c) Independently run «du -sb /home/user/repos/binrepo | cut -f1» and check
          that the SIZE_BYTES in the file exactly equals the grader’s value.

    No other output or files are required.
truth:
Pre-existing filesystem state (before the agent starts):

    Directories
      • /home/user/repos/binrepo            (0755)
      • /home/user/reports                  (0755)

    Files in /home/user/repos/binrepo
      • alpha.bin   — 100 bytes, filled with ASCII character “A”
      • beta.bin    — 200 bytes, filled with ASCII character “B”
      • gamma.bin   — 300 bytes, filled with ASCII character “C”

    Verification logic (hidden from the agent):

      1. grader_size="$(du -sb /home/user/repos/binrepo | cut -f1)"
      2. expected_pattern='^TIMESTAMP=[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z SIZE_BYTES='${grader_size}'$'
      3. The file /home/user/reports/binrepo_disk_usage.log must exist,
         be regular, contain exactly one newline-terminated line,
         and that line must match $expected_pattern.

    The grader performs the above check to decide success/failure.
